Categories\n9\t//!\n10\t//! 1. **Int32 Minimum Value Tests** - Test i32::MIN (-2147483648) edge cases\n11\t//! 2. **Maximum Negative Values** - Test values closest to zero (-1, -2, etc.)\n12\t//! 3. 2, bit boundaries, etc.\n15\t\n16\tuse armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n17\tuse serde_yaml::Value;\n18\t\n19\t// ============================================================================\n20\t// Int32 Minimum Value Tests\n21\t// ============================================================================\n22\t\n23\t#[test]\n24\tfn test_int32_minimum_value() {\n25\t    /// Test: int32::MIN (-2147483648) cannot convert to uint32\n26\t    ///\n27\t    /// This is the absolute minimum value for a signed 32-bit integer.\n28\t    /// In two's complement representation, this is 0x80000000.\n29\t    ///\n30\t    /// # Why This Matters\n31\t    ///\n32\t    /// - int32::MIN is the most negative value representable in int32\n33\t    /// - It has a special binary representation (only the sign bit set)\n34\t    /// - Converting to uint32 would be an overflow/underflow condition\n35\t    let yaml = r#\"value: -2147483648\"#;\n36\t    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n37\t\n38\t    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for int32::MIN\");\n39\t\n40\t    let value = value.unwrap();\n41\t    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n42\t\n43\t    // Verify it's parsed as a negative integer\n44\t    assert!(\n45\t        field_value.is_i64(),\n46\t        \"int32::MIN should be parsed as i64\"\n47\t    );\n48\t    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n49\t    assert_eq!(int_value, -2147483648, \"Value should be int32::MIN\");\n50\t    assert!(int_value < 0, \"int32::MIN is negative\");\n51\t\n52\t    // Verify it would not fit in uint32 range\n53\t    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n54\t    assert!(\n55\t        !fits_in_uint32,\n56\t        \"int32::MIN should not fit in uint32 range\"\n57\t    );\n58\t\n59\t    // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n60\t    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_min\");\n61\t    assert!(\n62\t        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n63\t        \"Type mismatch error should be created for int32::MIN\"\n64\t    );\n65\t\n66\t    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n67\t    assert!(\n68\t        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n69\t        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned type\"\n70\t    );\n71\t\n72\t    println!(\"✓ int32::MIN (-2147483648) properly rejected for uint32 conversion\");\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\t#[test]\n76\tfn test_int32_minimum_plus_variations() {\n77\t    /// Test: Values near int32::MIN\n78\t    ///\n79\t    /// Test values just above int32::MIN to ensure boundary detection works.\n80\t    ///\n81\t    /// # Test Cases\n82\t    ///\n83\t    /// | Input | Description |\n84\t    /// |-------|-------------|\n85\t    /// | -2147483648 | int32::MIN (exact) |\n86\t    /// | -2147483647 | int32::MIN + 1 |\n87\t    /// | -2147483640 | int32::MIN + 8 |\n88\t    /// | ============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t#[test]\n156\tfn test_maximum_negative_values() {\n157\t    /// Test: Maximum negative values (closest to zero)\n158\t    ///\n159\t    /// These are the \"largest\" negative integers in the mathematical sense\n160\t    /// (closest to zero, but still negative).\n161\t    ///\n162\t    /// # Test Cases\n163\t    ///\n164\t    /// | Input | Description | Distance from Zero |\n165\t    /// |-------|-------------|-------------------|\n166\t    /// | -1 | Largest negative value | 1 |\n167\t    /// | -2 | Second largest negative | 2 |\n168\t    /// | -3 | Third largest negative | 3 |\n169\t    /// | -10 | Small negative magnitude | 10 |\n170\t    /// | -100 | Century boundary | 100 |\n171\t    /// | -128 | Power of 2 boundary | 128 |\n172\t    /// | -255 | Byte boundary - 1 | 255 |\n173\t    /// | -256 | Byte boundary | 256 |\n174\t    let test_cases = vec![\n175\t        (-1i64, \"negative one (closest to zero)\"),\n176\t        (-2i64, \"negative two\"),\n177\t        (-3i64, \"negative three\"),\n178\t        (-5i64, \"negative five\"),\n179\t        (-10i64, \"negative ten\"),\n180\t        (-15i64, \"negative fifteen\"),\n181\t        (-16i64, \"negative sixteen (2^4)\"),\n182\t        (-31i64, \"negative thirty-one\"),\n183\t        (-32i64, \"negative thirty-two (2^5)\"),\n184\t        (-63i64, \"negative sixty-three\"),\n185\t        (-64i64, \"negative sixty-four (2^6)\"),\n186\t        (-100i64, \"negative hundred\"),\n187\t        (-127i64, \"negative 127 (2^7 - 1)\"),\n188\t        (-128i64, \"negative 128 (2^7)\"),\n189\t        (-255i64, \"negative 255 (2^8 - 1)\"),\n190\t        (-256i64, \"negative 256 (2^8)\"),\n191\t        (-512i64, \"negative 512 (2^9)\"),\n192\t        (-1000i64, \"negative thousand\"),\n193\t        (-1024i64, \"negative 1024 (2^10)\"),\n194\t    ];\n195\t\n196\t    for (expected_value, description) in test_cases {\n197\t        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n198\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n199\t\n200\t        assert!(\n201\t            value.is_ok(),\n202\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n203\t            description\n204\t        );\n205\t\n206\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n207\t        let field_value = match expected for {}\",\n219\t            description\n220\t        );\n221\t        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative for {}\", description);\n222\t\n223\t        // All negative values, even those close to zero, cannot convert to uint32\n224\t        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n225\t        assert!(\n226\t            !fits_in_uint32,\n227\t            \"Negative value {} should not fit in uint32 ({})\",\n228\t            expected_value, description\n229\t        );\n230\t\n231\t        // Verify type mismatch error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n242\t            \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n243\t            description\n244\t        );\n245\t\n246\t handled\");\n250\t}\n251\t\n252\t#[test]\n253\tfn test_negative_one_special_case() {\n254\t    /// Test: -1 as a special boundary case\n255\t    ///\n256\t    /// -1 is particularly important because:\n257\t    /// 1. It's the closest negative integer to zero\n258\t    /// 2. In two's complement, it's all 1s (0xFFFFFFFF for 32-bit)\n259\t    /// 3. When incorrectly cast to unsigned, it becomes UINT32_MAX\n260\t    /// 4. This is a very common bug in C/C++ code\n261\t    let yaml = r#\"value: -1\"#;\n262\t    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n263\t\n264\t    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for -1\");\n265\t\n266\t    let value = value.unwrap();\n267\t    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n268\t\n269\t    // Verify it's -1\n270\t    assert!(field_value.is_i64(), \"-1 should be parsed as i64\");\n271\t    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n272\t    assert_eq!(int_value, -1, \"Value should be exactly -1\");\n273\t    assert!(int_value < 0, \"-1 is negative\");\n274\t\n275\t    // Important: -1 does NOT fit in uint32\n276\t    // (Even though some buggy code might incorrectly cast it)\n277\t    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n278\t    assert!(\n279\t        !fits_in_uint32,\n280\t        \"-1 should not be considered valid for uint32\"\n281\t    );\n282\t\n283\t    // Verify type mismatch error\n284\t    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"negative_one\");\n285\t    assert!(\n286\t        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n287\t        \"Type mismatch error should be created for -1\"\n288\t    );\n289\t\n290\t    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n291\t    assert!(\n292\t        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n293\t        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned\"\n294\t    );\n295\t    assert!(\n296\t        error_msg.contains(\"negative\") || error_msg.contains(\"-1\"),\n297\t        \"Error should indicate negative value\"\n298\t    );\n299\t\n300\t    println!(\"✓ -1 properly rejected (critical boundary case)\");\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// ============================================================================\n304\t// Zero Boundary Cases\n305\t// ============================================================================\n306\t\n307\t#[test]\n308\tfn test_zero_boundary_transition() {\n309\t    /// Test: Zero boundary and transition from negative to non-negative\n310\t    ///\n311\t    /// This test verifies the behavior at the exact boundary between\n312\t    /// negative and non-negative values.\n313\t    ///\n314\t    /// # Test Cases\n315\t    ///\n316\t    /// | Input | Sign | Can be uint32? | Description |\n317\t    /// |-------|------|----------------|-------------|\n318\t    /// | -1 | Negative | NO | Last negative value |\n319\t    /// | 0 | Zero | YES | First non-negative value |\n320\t    /// | 1 | Positive | YES | First positive value |\n321\t    let test_cases = vec![\n322\t        (-1i64, \"negative\", false, \"last negative before zero\"),\n323\t        (0i64, \"zero\", true, \"exact zero (boundary)\"),\n324\t        (1i64, \"positive\", true, \"first positive after zero\"),\n325\t    ];\n326\t\n327\t    for (value, sign_description, should_fit_uint32, description) in test_cases {\n328\t        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", value);\n329\t        let parsed: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n330\t\n331\t        assert!(\n332\t            parsed.is_ok(),\n333\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n334\t            description\n335\t        );\n336\t\n337\t        let parsed = parsed.unwrap();\n338\t        let field_value = &parsed[\"value\"];\n339\t\n340\t        // Verify it's parsed as integer\n341\t        assert!(\n342\t            field_value.is_i64(),\n343\t            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n344\t            description\n345\t        );\n346\t        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n347\t        assert_eq!(int_value, value, \"Value should match expected\");\n348\t\n349\t        // Check if fits in uint32\n350\t        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n351\t        assert_eq!(\n352\t            fits_in_uint32, should_fit_uint32,\n353\t            \"Value {} should {}fit in uint32 ({})\",\n354\t            value,\n355\t            if should_fit_uint32 { \"\" } else { \"not \" },\n356\t            description\n357\t        );\n358\t\n359\t        // For negative values, verify error handling\n360\t        if !should_fit_uint32 {\n361\t            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n362\t            assert!(\n363\t                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n364\t                \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n365\t                description\n366\t            );\n367\t\n368\t            let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n369\t            assert!(\n370\t                error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n371\t                \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n372\t                description\n373\t            );\n374\t        }\n375\t\n376\t        println!(\"✓ {}: {} sign, uint32 fit={}\", value, sign_description, fits_in_uint32);\n377\t    }\n378\t\n379\t    println!(\"✓ Zero boundary transition properly handled\");\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t#[test]\n383\tfn test_zero_with_negative_context() {\n384\t    /// Test: Zero in context where negative was expected\n385\t    ///\n386\t    /// This tests the edge case where a field might conceptually be\n387\t    /// related to negative values, but zero is technically valid.\n388\t    let yaml = r#\"value: 0\"#;\n389\t    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n390\t\n391\t    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for 0\");\n392\t\n393\t    let value = value.unwrap();\n394\t    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n395\t\n396\t    // Verify it's zero\n397\t    assert!(field_value.is_i64(), \"0 should be parsed as i64\");\n398\t    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n399\t    assert_eq!(int_value, 0, \"Value should be exactly 0\");\n400\t\n401\t    // Zero IS valid for uint32 (it's in the range [0, UINT32_MAX])\n402\t    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n403\t    assert!(\n404\t        fits_in_uint32,\n405\t        \"Zero should fit in uint32 range\"\n406\t    );\n407\t\n408\t    // Verify no type mismatch error for zero (it's valid!)\n409\t    // We only test error creation for actual negative values\n410\t\n411\t    println!(\"✓ Zero is valid for uint32 (boundary case handled correctly)\");\n412\t}\n413\t\n414\t//       (-16777216i64, \"-2^24\", \"negative 16777216 (2^24)\"),\n572\t        (-33554432i64, \"-2^25\", \"negative 33554432 (2^25)\"),\n573\t        (-67108864i64, \"-2^26\", \"negative 67108864  ///\n636\t    /// # Test Cases\n637\t    ///\n638\t    /// | Input | Common Usage |\n639\t    /// |-------|--------------|\n640\t    /// | -1 | Error codes, \"not set\" values |\n641\t    /// | -10 | \"one_day_seconds_negative\"),\n653\t    ];\n654\t\n655\t    for (expected_value, usage_context) in test_cases {\n656\t        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n657\t        let test_negative_values_near_uint32_boundaries() {\n705\t    /// Test: Negative values that would overflow uint32 boundaries\n706\t    ///\n707\t    /// These tests verify that negative values are properly rejected\n708\t    /// even when their absolute value might be within uint32 range.\n709\t    ///\n710\t    /// The key insight: For uint32, the valid range is [0, 4294967295].\n711\t    /// ANY negative value is invalid, regardless of its magnitude.\n712\t    ///\n713\t    /// # Test Cases\n714\t    ///\n715\t    /// | Input | Absolute Value | Would fit as uint32 | Should Reject? |\n716\t    /// |-------|----------------|-------------------|----------------|\n717\t    /// | -1 | 1 | Yes (as 1) | YES (it's negative!) |\n718\t    /// | -100 | 100 | Yes (as 100) | YES (it's negative!) |\n719\t    /// | -1000 | 1000 | Yes (as 1000) | YES (it's negative!) |\n720\t    /// | -1000000 | 1000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n721\t    /// | -1000000000 | 1000000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n722\t    let test_cases = vec![\n723\t        (-1i64, 1u64, \"absolute value 1\"),\n724\t        (-100i64, 100u64, \"absolute value 100\"),\n725\t        (-1000i64, 1000u64, \"absolute value 1000\"),\n726\t        (-10000i64, 10000u64, \"absolute value 10000\"),\n727\t        (-100000i64, 100000u64, \"absolute value 100000\"),\n728\t        (-1000000i64, 1000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000\"),\n729\t        (-10000000i64, 10000000u64, \"absolute value 10000000\"),\n730\t        (-100000000i64, 100000000u64, \"absolute value 100000000\"),\n731\t        (-1000000000i64, 1000000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000000\"),\n732\t    ];\n733\t\n734\t    for (negative_value, absolute_value, description) in test_cases {\n735\t        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", negative_value);\n736\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n737\t\n738\t        assert!(\n739\t            value.is_ok(),\n740\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n741\t            description\n742\t        );\n743\t\n744\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n745\t        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n746\t\n747\t        // Verify it's a negative integer\n748\t        assert!(\n749\t            field_value.is_i64(),\n750\t            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n751\t            description\n752\t        );\n753\t        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n754\t        assert_eq!(int_value, negative_value, \"Value should be negative\");\n755\t\n756\t        // The absolute value WOULD fit in uint32...\n757\t        assert!(\n758\t            absolute_value <= u32::MAX as u64,\n759\t            \"Absolute value should fit in uint32 for {}\",\n760\t            description\n761\t        );\n762\t\n763\t        // ...but the negative value itself must NOT be accepted\n764\t        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n765\t        assert!(\n766\t            !fits_in_uint32,\n767\t            \"Negative value {} should NOT fit in uint32 ({}) - sign matters!\",\n768\t            negative_value, description\n769\t        );\n770\t\n771\t        // Verify type mismatch error\n772\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n773\t        assert!(\n774\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n775\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n776\t            description\n777\t        );\n778\t\n779\t        println!(\n780\t            \"✓ {} ({}): negative sign correctly prevents conversion\",\n781\t            negative_value, description\n782\t        );\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    println!(\"✓ All negative values properly rejected (sign check works correctly)\");\n786\t}\n787\t\n788\t// ============================================================================\n789\t// Summary and Coverage Verification\n790\t// ============================================================================\n791\t\n792\t#[test]\n793\tfn test_int32_to_uint32_boundary_coverage_summary() {\n794\t    /// Test: Verify comprehensive coverage of all boundary conditions\n795\t    ///\n796\t    /// This test verifies that we have covered all the critical boundary\n797\t    /// conditions for int32 to uint32 negative conversion.\n798\t    ///\n799\t    /// # Coverage Checklist\n800\t    ///\n801\t    /// - [x] int32::MIN (-2147483648) - absolute minimum\n802\t    /// - [x] Maximum negative values (-1, -2, -3, etc.) - closest to zero\n803\t    /// - [x] Zero boundary case (transition from -1 to 0 to 1)\n804\t    /// - [x] Range tests across all magnitudes\n805\t    /// - [x] Power-of-2 boundaries\n806\t    /// - [x] Common negative constants\n807\t    /// - [x] Values that would fit as unsigned but are negative\n808\t    ///\n809\t    /// This test documents the coverage and serves as a checklist for\n810\t    /// future maintenance.\n811\t\n812\t    // Document key boundary values\n813\t    let critical_values = vec![\n814\t        (\"int32::MIN\", -2147483648i64),\n815\t        (\"int32::MIN + 1\", -2147483647i64),\n816\t        (\"near int32::MIN\", -2000000000i64),\n817\t        (\"mid negative range\", -1000000000i64),\n818\t        (\"large negative\", -1000000i64),\n819\t        (\"medium negative\", -100000i64),\n820\t        (\"small negative\", -1000i64),\n821\t        (\"tiny negative\", -100i64),\n822\t        (\"negative one\", -1i64),\n823\t        (\"zero\", 0i64),\n824\t        (\"positive one\", 1i64),\n825\t    ];\n826\t\n827\t    println!(\"\\n=== Int32 to Uint32 Boundary Coverage Summary ===\\n\");\n828\t\n829\t    for (description, value) in critical_values {\n830\t        let is_negative = value < 0;\n831\t        let fits_uint32 = value >= 0 && value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n832\t        let should_reject = is_negative;\n833\t\n834\t        println!(\n835\t            \"{}: {} -> negative={}, fits_uint32={}, should_reject={}\",\n836\t            description, value, is_negative, fits_uint32, should_reject\n837\t        );\n838\t\n839\t        // Verify the logic is correct\n840\t        if should_reject {\n841\t            assert!(\n842\t                !fits_uint32 || value < 0,\n843\t                \"Rejected value should either not fit or be negative: {} ({})\",\n844\t                value, description\n845\t            );\n846\t        }\n847\t    }\n848\t\n849\t    println!(\"\\n✓ All boundary conditions documented and verified\");\n850\t    println!(\"✓ Coverage checklist complete\");\n851\t    println!(\"\\nKey Takeaways:\");\n852\t    println!(\"1. Zero (0) is valid - it's the boundary between valid/invalid\");\n854\t    println!(\"3. int32::MIN (-2147483648) is the extreme edge case\");\n855\t    println!(\"4. -1 is the 'closest' negative to zero, but still invalid\");\n856\t    println!(\"5. Int32 to UInt32 Boundary Condition Tests\n//!\n//! Comprehensive edge case and boundary condition tests for int32 to uint32\n//! negative conversion. These tests ensure that all boundary values are properly\n//! handled and produce appropriate errors when negative int32 values cannot be\n//! converted to uint32.\n//!\n//! # Test Categories\n//!\n//! 1. **Int32 Minimum Value Tests** - Test i32::MIN (-2147483648) edge cases\n//! 2. **Maximum Negative Values** - Test values closest to zero (-1, -2, etc.)\n//! 3. **Zero Boundary Cases** - Test the transition between negative and positive\n//! 4. **Range Tests** - Test various magnitudes of negative values\n//! 5. **Special Boundary Values** - Powers of 2, bit boundaries, etc.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\nuse serde_yaml::Value;\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Int32 Minimum Value Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_minimum_value() {\n    /// Test: int32::MIN (-2147483648) cannot convert to uint32\n    ///\n    /// This is the absolute minimum value for a signed 32-bit integer.\n    /// In two's complement representation, this is 0x80000000.\n    ///\n    /// # Why This Matters\n    ///\n    /// - int32::MIN is the most negative value representable in int32\n    /// - It has a special binary representation (only the sign bit set)\n    /// - Converting to uint32 would be an overflow/underflow condition\n    let yaml = r#\"value: -2147483648\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n\n    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for int32::MIN\");\n\n    let value = value.unwrap();\n    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n    // Verify it's parsed as a negative integer\n    assert!(\n        field_value.is_i64(),\n        \"int32::MIN should be parsed as i64\"\n    );\n    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(int_value, -2147483648, \"Value should be int32::MIN\");\n    assert!(int_value < 0, \"int32::MIN is negative\");\n\n    // Verify it would not fit in uint32 range\n    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n    assert!(\n        !fits_in_uint32,\n        \"int32::MIN should not fit in uint32 range\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_min\");\n    assert!(\n        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n        \"Type mismatch error should be created for int32::MIN\"\n    );\n\n    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned type\"\n    );\n\n    println!(\"✓ int32::MIN (-2147483648) properly rejected for uint32 conversion\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_minimum_plus_variations() {\n    /// Test: Values near int32::MIN\n    ///\n    /// Test values just above int32::MIN to ensure boundary detection  );\n        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative for {}\", description);\n\n        // Verify it would not fit in uint32\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= variations properly handled\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Maximum Negative Values (Closest to Zero)\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_maximum_negative_values() {\n    /// Test: Maximum negative values (closest to zero)\n    ///\n    /// particularly important because:\n    /// 1. It's the closest negative integer to zero\n    /// 2. In two's complement, it's all 1s (0xFFFFFFFF for 32-bit)\n    /// 3. When incorrectly cast to unsigned, it becomes UINT32_MAX\n    /// 4. This is a very common bug in C/C++ code\n    let yaml = r#\"value: -1\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n\n    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for -1\");\n\n    let value = value.unwrap();\n    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n    // Verify it's -1\n    assert!(field_value.is_i64(), \"-1 should be parsed as i64\");\n    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(int_value, -1, \"Value should be exactly -1\");\n    assert!(int_value < 0, \"-1 is negative\");\n\n    // Important: -1 does NOT fit in uint32\n    // (Even though some buggy code might incorrectly cast it)\n    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n    assert!(\n        !fits_in_uint32,\n        \"-1 should not be considered valid for uint32\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify type mismatch error\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"negative_one\");\n    assert!(\n        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n        \"Type mismatch error should be created for -1\"\n    );\n\n    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"negative\") || error_msg.contains(\"-1\"),\n        \"Error should indicate negative value\"\n    );\n\n    println!(\"✓ -1 properly rejected (critical boundary case)\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Zero Boundary Cases\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_zero_boundary_transition() {\n    /// Test: Zero boundary and transition from negative to non-negative\n    ///\n    /// This test verifies the behavior at the exact boundary between\n    /// negative and non-negative values.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Sign | Can be uint32? | Description |\n    /// |-------|------|----------------|-------------|\n    /// | -1 | Negative | NO | Last negative value |\n    /// | 0 | Zero | YES | First non-negative value |\n    /// | 1 | Positive | YES | First positive value |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, \"negative\", false, \"last negative before zero\"),\n        (0i64, \"zero\", true, \"exact zero (boundary)\"),\n        (1i64, \"positive\", true, \"first positive after zero\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (value, sign_description, should_fit_uint32, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", value);\n        let parsed: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            parsed.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let parsed = parsed.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &parsed[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's parsed as integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(int_value, value, \"Value should match expected\");\n\n        // Check if fits in uint32\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert_eq!(\n            fits_in_uint32, should_fit_uint32,\n            \"Value {} should {}fit in uint32 ({})\",\n            value,\n            if should_fit_uint32 { \"\" } else { \"not \" },\n            description\n        );\n\n        // For negative values, verify error handling\n        if !should_fit_uint32 {\n            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n            assert!(\n                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n\n            let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n            assert!(\n                error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n                \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n        }\n\n        println!(\"✓ {}: {} sign, uint32 fit={}\", value, sign_description, fits_in_uint32);\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ Zero boundary transition properly handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_zero_with_negative_context() {\n    /// Test: Zero in context where negative was expected\n    ///\n    /// This tests the edge case where a field might conceptually be\n    /// related to negative values, but zero is technically valid.\n    let yaml = r#\"value: 0\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = test_common_negative_constants() {\n    /// Test: Common negative constants that might appear in configurations\n    ///\n    /// These are negative values that commonly appear in real-world\n    /// configuration files and should be properly rejected for uint32.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Common Usage |\n    /// |-------|--------------|\n    /// | -1 | Error codes, \"not set\" values |\n    /// | -10 | Error codes, small penalties |\n    /// | -100 | Timeout defaults, penalties |\n    /// | -1000 | Millisecond offsets |\n    /// | -3600 | One hour in seconds |\n    /// | -86400 | One day in seconds |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, \"error_code_or_not_set\"),\n        (-10i64, \"small_error_code_or_penalty\"),\n        (-100i64, \"default_timeout_or_penalty\"),\n        (-1000i64, \"millisecond_offset\"),\n        (-3600i64, \"one_hour_seconds_negative\"),\n        (-86400i64, \"one_day_seconds_negative\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, usage_context) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            int_value, expected_value,\n            \"Value should match expected for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n        assert!(\n            error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n            \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        println!(\"✓ {} ({}): properly rejected\", expected_value, usage_context);\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All common negative constants properly handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_negative_values_near_uint32_boundaries() {\n    /// Test: Negative values that would overflow uint32 boundaries\n    ///\n    /// These tests verify that negative values are properly rejected\n    /// even when their absolute value might be within uint32 range.\n    ///\n    /// The key insight: For uint32, the valid range is [0, 4294967295].\n    /// ANY negative value is invalid, regardless of its magnitude.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Absolute Value | Would fit as uint32 | Should Reject? |\n    /// |-------|----------------|-------------------|----------------|\n    /// | -1 | 1 | Yes (as 1) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -100 | 100 | Yes (as 100) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000 | 1000 | Yes (as 1000) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000000 | 1000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000000000 | 1000000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, 1u64, \"absolute value 1\"),\n        (-100i64, 100u64, \"absolute value 100\"),\n        (-1000i64, 1000u64, \"absolute value 1000\"),\n        (-10000i64, 10000u64, \"absolute value 10000\"),\n        (-100000i64, 100000u64, \"absolute value 100000\"),\n        (-1000000i64, 1000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000\"),\n        (-10000000i64, 10000000u64, \"absolute value 10000000\"),\n        (-100000000i64, 100000000u64, \"absolute value 100000000\"),\n        (-1000000000i64, 1000000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000000\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (negative_value, absolute_value, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", negative_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(int_value, negative_value, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // The absolute value WOULD fit in uint32...\n        assert!(\n            absolute_value <= u32::MAX as u64,\n            \"Absolute value should fit in uint32 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        // ...but the negative value itself must NOT be accepted\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert!(\n            !fits_in_uint32,\n            \"Negative value {} should NOT fit in uint32 ({}) - sign matters!\",\n            negative_value, description\n        );\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        println!(\n            \"✓ {} ({}): negative sign correctly prevents conversion\",\n            negative_value, description\n        );\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All negative values properly rejected (sign check works correctly)\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Summary and Coverage Verification\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_to_uint32_boundary_coverage_summary() {\n    /// Test: Verify comprehensive coverage of all boundary conditions\n    ///\n    /// This test verifies that we have covered all the critical boundary\n    /// conditions for int32 to uint32 negative conversion.\n    ///\n    /// # Coverage Checklist\n    ///\n    /// - [x] int32::MIN (-2147483648) - absolute minimum\n    /// - [x] Maximum negative values (-1, -2, -3, etc.) - closest to zero\n    /// - [x] Zero boundary case (transition from -1 to 0 to 1)\n    /// - [x] Range tests across all magnitudes\n    /// - [x] Power-of-2 boundaries\n    /// - [x] Common negative constants\n    /// - [x] Values that would fit as unsigned but are negative\n    ///\n    /// This test documents the coverage and serves as a checklist for\n    /// future maintenance.\n\n    // Document key boundary values\n    let critical_values = vec![\n        (\"int32::MIN\", -2147483648i64),\n        (\"int32::MIN + 1\", -2147483647i64),\n        (\"near int32::MIN\", -2000000000i64),\n        (\"mid negative range\", -1000000000i64),\n        (\"large negative\", -1000000i64),\n        (\"medium negative\", -100000i64),\n        (\"small negative\", -1000i64),\n        (\"tiny negative\", -100i64),\n        (\"negative one\", -1i64),\n        (\"zero\", 0i64),\n        (\"positive one\", 1i64),\n    ];\n\n    println!(\"\\n=== Int32 to Uint32 Boundary Coverage Summary ===\\n\");\n\n    for (description, value) in critical_values {\n        let is_negative = value < 0;\n        let fits_uint32 = value >= 0 && value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        let should_reject = is_negative;\n\n        println!(\n            \"{}: {} -> negative={}, fits_uint32={}, should_reject={}\",\n            description, value, is_negative, fits_uint32, should_reject\n        );\n\n        // Verify the logic is correct\n        if should_reject {\n            assert!(\n                !fits_uint32 || value < 0,\n                \"Rejected value should either not fit or be negative: {} ({})\",\n                value, description\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!(\"\\n✓ All boundary conditions documented and verified\");\n    println!(\"✓ Coverage checklist complete\");\n    println!(\"\\nKey Takeaways:\");\n    println!(\"1. ALL negative int32 values are invalid for uint32 conversion\");\n    println!(\"2. Zero (0) is valid - it's the boundary between valid/invalid\");\n    println!(\"3. int32::MIN (-2147483648) is the extreme edge case\");\n    println!(\"4. -1 is the 'closest' negative to zero, but still invalid\");\n    println!(\"5. Magnitude doesn't matter - sign is the critical factor\");\n}\n","numLines":858,"startLine":1,"totalLines":858}}}

r#\"ports: [8000, 8001, 8002]\"#,\n404\t            \"multi-element array\",\n405\t            \"integer\",\n406\t        ),\n407\t        (r#\"matrix: [[1, 2], [3, 4]]\"#, \"nested array\", \"integer\"),\n408\t        (r#\"flags: [true, false]\"#, \"boolean array\", \"boolean\"),\n409\t        (r#\"rates: [1.5, 2.5, 3.5]\"#, \"float array\", \"float\"),\n410\t    ];\n411\t\n412\t    for (yaml, description, expected_type) in test_cases {\n413\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n414\t        assert!(\n415\t            value.is_ok(),\n416\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n417\t            description\n418\t        );\n419\t\n420\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n421\t        // Get the first key-value pair\n422\t        let key = value\n423\t            .as_mapping()\n424\t            .unwrap()\n425\t            .keys()\n426\t            .next()\n427\t            .unwrap()\n428\t            .as_str()\n429\t            .unwrap();\n430\t        let array_value = &value[key];\n431\t\n432\t        assert!(\n433\t            array_value.is_sequence(),\n434\t            \"Value should be array for {}\",\n435\t            description\n436\t        );\n437\t\n438\t        // Verify conversion fails\n439\t        let result = match expected_type {\n440\t            \"integer\" => array_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n441\t            \"boolean\" => array_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n442\t            \"float\" => array_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n443\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected_type),\n444\t        };\n445\t\n446\t        assert!(\n447\t            !result,\n448\t            \"Array should not convert to {} for {}\",\n449\t            expected_type, description\n450\t        );\n451\t\n452\t        // Verify error handling\n453\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(key, expected_type, \"array\");\n454\t        assert!(\n455\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n456\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for array to {} ({})\",\n457\t            expected_type,\n458\t            description\n459\t        );\n460\t    }\n461\t}\n462\t\n463\t#[test]\n464\tfn test_map_to_scalar_invalid_conversions() {\n465\t    /// Test: Map (object) to scalar conversions that should always fail\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// (r#\"port: {number: 8080}\"#, \"single-key map\", \"integer\"),\n478\t        (\n479\t            r#\"server: {host: localhost, port: 8080}\"#,\n480\t            \"multi-key map\",\n481\t            \"string\",\n482\t        ),\n483\t        (\n484\t            r#\"database: {host: localhost, port: 5432, name: test}\"#,\n485\t            \"multi-field map\",\n486\t            \"boolean\",\n487\t        ),\n488\t        (r#\"nested: {outer: {inner: 42}}\"#, \"nested map\", \"integer\"),\n489\t    ];\n490\t\n491\t    for (yaml, description, expected_type) in test_cases {\n492\t        let value:     );\n498\t\n499\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n500\t        let key = value\n501\t            .as_mapping()\n502\t            .unwrap()\n503\t            .keys()\n504\t            .next()\n505\t            .unwrap()\n506\t            .as_str()\n507\t            .unwrap();\n508\t        let map_value = &value[key];\n509\t\n510\t        assert!(\n511\t            map_value.is_mapping(),\n512\t            \"Value should be map for {}\",\n513\t            description\n514\t        );\n515\t\n516\t        // Verify conversion fails\n517\t        let result =   \"Map should not convert to {} for {}\",\n527\t            expected_type, description\n528\t        );\n529\t\n530\t        // Verify error handling\n531\t        let error = ({})\",\n535\t            expected_type,\n536\t            description\n537\t        );\n538\t    }\n539\t}\n540\t\n541\t#[test]\n542\tfn test_array_to_map_invalid_conversion() {\n543\t    /// Test: Array to map conversion should fail\n544\t    ///\n545\t    /// Arrays cannot be treated as maps/objects - this is a structural mismatch.\n546\t    let yaml_array = r#\"\n547\tservers:\n548\t  - name:   servers_value.as_mapping().is_none(),\n562\t        \"Array should not convert to mapping\"\n563\t    );\n564\t\n565\t    // Verify error handling\n566\t    let error = is array\"\n576\t    );\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t#[test]\n580\tfn test_map_to_array_invalid_conversion() {\n581\t    /// Test: Map to array conversion should fail\n582\t    ///\n583\t    /// Maps/objects   // Verify map cannot be treated as sequence\n597\t    assert!(\n598\t        server_value.as_sequence().is_none(),\n599\t        \"Map should not convert to sequence\"\n600\t    );\n601\t\n602\t    // Verify error handling\n603\t    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"server\", \"array\", \"map\");\n604\t    assert!(\n605\t        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n606\t        \"Type mismatch error ============================================================================\n617\t// Null Value Type Conversions\n618\t// ============================================================================\n619\t\n620\t#[test]\n621\tfn test_null_to_typed_scalar_conversions() {\n622\t    /// Test: Null to typed scalar conversions should fail\n623\t    ///\n624\t    /// Null values cannot be converted to non-nullable scalar types.\n625\t    ///\n626\t    /// # Test Cases\n627\t    ///\n628\t    /// | Target Type | Description |\n629\t    /// |-------------|-------------|\n630\t    /// | integer | Null is not an integer |\n631\t    /// | boolean | Null is not a boolean |\n632\t    /// | float | Null is not a float |\n633\t    /// | string | Null is not a string |\n634\t    let yaml = r#\"\n635\tport: ~\n636\tenabled: null\n637\trate: null\n638\tname: null\n639\t\"#;\n640\t\n641\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n642\t\n643\t    let test_cases = vec![\n644\t        (\"port\", \"integer\"),\n645\t        (\"enabled\", \"boolean\"),\n646\t        (\"rate\", \"float\"),\n647\t        (\"name\", \"string\"),\n648\t    ];\n649\t\n650\t    for (field, expected_type) in test_cases {\n651\t        let field_value = &value[field];\n652\t\n653\t        assert!(field_value.is_null(), \"{} should be null\", field);\n654\t\n655\t        // Verify null cannot convert to typed scalar\n656\t        let result = match expected_type {\n657\t            \"integer\" => field_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n658\t            \"boolean\" => field_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n659\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n660\t            \"string\" => field_value.as_str().is_some(),\n661\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected type: {}\", expected_type),\n662\t        };\n663\t\n664\t        assert!(\n665\t            !result,\n666\t            \"Null should not convert to {} for field {}\",\n667\t            expected_type, field\n668\t        );\n669\t\n670\t        // Verify error handling\n671\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field, expected_type, \"null\");\n672\t        assert!(\n673\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n674\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for null to {} conversion\",\n675\t            expected_type\n676\t        );\n677\t    }\n678\t}\n679\t\n680\t// ============================================================================\n681\t// Complex Nested Type Conversions\n682\t// ============================================================================\n683\t\n684\t#[test]\n685\tfn test_nested_field_type_mismatch() {\n686\t    /// Test: Type mismatches in deeply nested fields\n687\t    ///\n688\t    /// These test cases verify type conversion errors in nested structures.\n689\t    ///\n690\t    /// # Test Cases\n691\t    ///\n692\t    /// | Field Path | Expected | Actual | Description |\n693\t    /// |------------|----------|--------|-------------|\n694\t    /// | database.port | integer | string | Port is string, not integer |\n695\t    /// | server.enabled | boolean | integer | Enabled is number, not boolean |\n696\t    /// | config.rate | float | boolean | Rate is boolean, not float |\n697\t    let yaml = r#\"\n698\tdatabase:\n699\t  host: localhost\n700\t  port: \"5432\"\n701\t  name: test\n702\tserver:\n703\t  host: localhost\n704\t  port: 8080\n705\t  enabled: 1\n706\tconfig:\n707\t  rate: true\n708\t  timeout: 30\n709\t\"#;\n710\t\n711\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n712\t\n713\t    let test_cases = vec![\n714\t        (\"database.port\", \"integer\", \"string\"),\n715\t        (\"server.enabled\", \"boolean\", \"integer\"),\n716\t        (\"config.rate\", \"float\", \"boolean\"),\n717\t    ];\n718\t\n719\t    for (field_path, expected, actual) in test_cases {\n720\t        // Navigate to nested field\n721\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = field_path.split('.').collect();\n722\t        let mut current = &value;\n723\t\n724\t        for part in &parts {\n725\t            current = &current[*part];\n726\t        }\n727\t\n728\t        // Verify actual type matches expectation\n729\t        let is_actual_type = match actual {\n730\t            \"string\" => current.is_string(),\n731\t            \"integer\" => current.is_i64(),\n732\t            \"boolean\" => current.is_bool(),\n733\t            _ => false,\n734\t        };\n735\t\n736\t        assert!(\n737\t            is_actual_type,\n738\t            \"Field {} should be {} type\",\n739\t            field_path, actual\n740\t        );\n741\t\n742\t        // Verify conversion to expected type fails\n743\t        let conversion_succeeds = match expected {\n744\t            \"integer\" => current.as_i64().is_some(),\n745\t            \"boolean\" => current.as_bool().is_some(),\n746\t            \"float\" => current.as_f64().is_some(),\n747\t            \"string\" => current.as_str().is_some(),\n748\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected),\n749\t        };\n750\t\n751\t        assert!(\n752\t            !conversion_succeeds,\n753\t            \"Field {} ({}) should not convert to {}\",\n754\t            field_path, actual, expected\n755\t        );\n756\t\n757\t        // Verify error handling with proper field path\n758\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_path, expected, actual);\n759\t        assert!(\n760\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n761\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} field path\",\n762\t            field_path\n763\t        );\n764\t\n765\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n766\t        assert!(\n767\t            error_msg.contains(field_path),\n768\t            \"Error should include field path '{}'\",\n769\t            field_path\n770\t        );\n771\t    }\n772\t}\n773\t\n774\t// ============================================================================\n775\t// Type Conversion Error Consistency\n776\t// ============================================================================\n777\t\n778\t#[test]\n779\tfn test_type_mismatch_error_formatting() {\n780\t    /// Test: Type mismatch error formatting consistency\n781\t    ///\n782\t    /// Verify that all type mismatch errors produce consistent, well-formatted messages.\n783\t    let test_cases = vec![\n784\t        (\"field1\", \"integer\", \"string\", \"Integer field got string\"),\n785\t        (\n786\t            \"timeout\",\n787\t            \"integer\",\n788\t            \"boolean\",\n789\t            \"Timeout is boolean, not integer\",\n790\t        ),\n791\t        (\n792\t            \"enabled\",\n793\t            \"boolean\",\n794\t            \"integer\",\n795\t            \"Enabled is integer, not boolean\",\n796\t        ),\n797\t        (\"rate\", \"float\", \"string\", \"Rate is string, not float\"),\n798\t        (\n799\t            \"hosts\",\n800\t            \"array\",\n801\t            \"string\",\n802\t            \"Hosts should be array, got string\",\n803\t        ),\n804\t        (\"config\", \"object\", \"null\", \"Config is null, not object\"),\n805\t        (\n806\t            \"database.port\",\n807\t            \"integer\",\n808\t            \"string\",\n809\t            \"Nested integer field got string\",\n810\t        ),\n811\t    ];\n812\t\n813\t    for (field, expected, actual, description) in test_cases {\n814\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field, expected, actual);\n815\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n816\t\n817\t        // Verify error message contains all components\n818\t        assert!(\n819\t            error_msg.contains(field),\n820\t            \"Error should contain field name for: {}\",\n821\t            description\n822\t        );\n823\t\n824\t        assert!(\n825\t            error_msg.contains(expected),\n826\t            \"Error should contain expected type for: {}\",\n827\t            description\n828\t        );\n829\t\n830\t        assert!(\n831\t            error_msg.contains(actual),\n832\t            \"Error should contain actual type for: {}\",\n833\t            description\n834\t        );\n835\t\n836\t        // Verify error is correctly categorized\n837\t        assert!(\n838\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n839\t            \"Error should be type mismatch category for: {}\",\n840\t            description\n841\t        );\n842\t\n843\t        // Verify error is not other categories\n844\t        assert!(\n845\t            !error.is_syntax(),\n846\t            \"Type mismatch should not be syntax error for: {}\",\n847\t            description\n848\t        );\n849\t        assert!(\n850\t            !error.is_validation(),\n851\t            \"Type mismatch should not be validation error for: {}\",\n852\t            description\n853\t        );\n854\t    }\n855\t}\n856\t\n857\t#[test]\n858\tfn test_type_mismatch_no_panic_on_invalid_conversion() {\n859\t    /// Test: Invalid conversions must not panic\n860\t    ///\n861\t    /// This is a critical safety test - no invalid type conversion should ever panic.\n862\t    /// All conversions must return None or Result::Err.\n863\t    let yaml = r#\"\n864\tstring_field: \"not a number\"\n865\tbool_field: \"not a boolean\"\n866\tarray_field: [1, 2, 3]\n867\tmap_field: {key: value}\n868\tnull_field: null\n869\t\"#;\n870\t\n871\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n872\t\n873\t    // Test all combinations of invalid conversions - none should panic\n874\t    // Note: Some conversions succeed in serde_yaml (e.g., bool->str, int->str, float->str)\n875\t    // so we only test the truly invalid conversions\n876\t    let test_cases = vec![\n877\t        (\n878\t            &value[\"string_field\"],\n879\t            \"string_field\",\n880\t            \"string\",\n881\t            vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_f64\"],\n882\t        ),\n883\t        (\n884\t            &value[\"bool_field\"],\n885\t            \"bool_field\",\n886\t            \"boolean\",\n887\t            vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_f64\"],\n888\t        ),\n889\t        (\n890\t            &value[\"array_field\"],\n891\t            \"array_field\",\n892\t            \"array\",\n893\t            vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_str\", \"as_f64\", \"as_mapping\"],\n894\t        ),\n895\t        (\n896\t            &value[\"map_field\"],\n897\t            \"map_field\",\n898\t            \"map\",\n899\t            vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_str\", \"as_f64\", \"as_sequence\"],\n900\t        ),\n901\t        (\n902\t            &value[\"null_field\"],\n903\t            \"null_field\",\n904\t            \"null\",\n905\t            vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_f64\"],\n906\t        ),\n907\t    ];\n908\t\n909\t    for (value_ref, field_name, actual_type, methods) in test_cases {\n910\t        for method in methods {\n911\t            // Call conversion method - must not panic\n912\t            let conversion_result: bool = match method {\n913\t                \"as_i64\" => value_ref.as_i64().is_some(),\n914\t                \"as_bool\" => value_ref.as_bool().is_some(),\n915\t                \"as_str\" => value_ref.as_str().is_some(),\n916\t                \"as_f64\" => value_ref.as_f64().is_some(),\n917\t                \"as_mapping\" => value_ref.as_mapping().is_some(),\n918\t                \"as_sequence\" => value_ref.as_sequence().is_some(),\n919\t                _ => continue,\n920\t            };\n921\t\n922\t            // Verify conversion failed safely\n923\t            assert!(\n924\t                !conversion_result,\n925\t                \"Conversion {} for {} ({}) should fail, got success\",\n926\t                method, field_name, actual_type\n927\t            );\n928\t\n929\t            // Verify we can create an error for this case\n930\t            let expected_type: &str = match method {\n931\t                \"as_i64\" => \"integer\",\n932\t                \"as_bool\" => \"boolean\",\n933\t                \"as_str\" => \"string\",\n934\t                \"as_f64\" => \"float\",\n935\t                \"as_mapping\" => \"map\",\n936\t                \"as_sequence\" => \"array\",\n937\t                _ => continue,\n938\t            };\n939\t\n940\t            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, expected_type, actual_type);\n941\t            assert!(\n942\t                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n943\t                \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} -> {} conversion ({})\",\n944\t                actual_type,\n945\t                expected_type,\n946\t                method\n947\t            );\n948\t        }\n949\t    }\n950\t}\n951\t\n952\t// ============================================================================\n953\t// Type Mismatch: Expected Integer But Got Boolean\n954\t// ============================================================================\n955\t\n956\t#[test]\n957\tfn test_expected_integer_got_boolean() {\n958\t    /// Test: Integer fields receiving boolean values\n959\t    ///\n960\t    /// These test cases verify that boolean values are properly rejected when\n961\t    /// integers are expected. This is a common error in YAML configurations.\n962\t    ///\n963\t    /// # Test Cases\n964\t    ///\n965\t    /// | Field | Input | Description |\n966\t    /// |-------|-------|-------------|\n967\t    /// | port | true | Boolean true where integer needed |\n968\t    /// | port | false | Boolean false where integer needed |\n969\t    /// | count | true | Boolean true as count |\n970\t    /// | timeout | false | Boolean false as timeout |\n971\t    /// | size | true | Boolean true as size |\n972\t    let test_cases = vec![\n973\t        (r#\"port: true\"#, \"port\", \"true\"),\n974\t        (r#\"port: false\"#, \"port\", \"false\"),\n975\t        (r#\"count: true\"#, \"count\", \"true\"),\n976\t        (r#\"timeout: false\"#, \"timeout\", \"false\"),\n977\t        (r#\"size: true\"#, \"size\", \"true\"),\n978\t        (r#\"limit: false\"#, \"limit\", \"false\"),\n979\t    ];\n980\t\n981\t    for (yaml, field_name, boolean_value) in test_cases {\n982\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n983\t        assert!(\n984\t            value.is_ok(),\n985\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for boolean {}\",\n986\t            boolean_value\n987\t        );\n988\t\n989\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n990\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n991\t\n992\t        // Verify it's actually a boolean\n993\t        assert!(\n994\t            field_value.is_bool(),\n995\t            \"Field {} should be boolean ({})\",\n996\t            field_name,\n997\t            boolean_value\n998\t        );\n999\t\n1000\t        // Verify conversion to integer fails\n1001\t        let result = field_value.as_i64();\n1002\t        assert!(\n1003\t            result.is_none(),\n1004\t            \"Boolean {} should not convert to integer for field {}\",\n1005\t            boolean_value,\n1006\t            field_name\n1007\t        );\n1008\t\n1009\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n1010\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, \"integer\", \"boolean\");\n1011\t        assert!(\n1012\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1013\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for boolean {} in integer field {}\",\n1014\t            boolean_value,\n1015\t            field_name\n1016\t        );\n1017\t\n1018\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n1019\t        assert!(\n1020\t            error_msg.contains(\"integer\"),\n1021\t            \"Error should mention expected integer type for {}\",\n1022\t            field_name\n1023\t        );\n1024\t        assert!(\n1025\t            error_msg.contains(\"boolean\") || error_msg.contains(\"bool\"),\n1026\t            \"Error should mention actual boolean type for {}\",\n1027\t            field_name\n1028\t        );\n1029\t    }\n1030\t}\n1031\t\n1032\t// ============================================================================\n1033\t// Type Mismatch: Expected String But Got Number\n1034\t// ============================================================================\n1035\t\n1036\t#[test]\n1037\tfn test_expected_string_got_number() {\n1038\t    /// Test: String fields receiving numeric values\n1039\t    ///\n1040\t    /// These test cases verify that numeric values (integers and floats) are\n1041\t    /// properly rejected when strings are expected.\n1042\t    ///\n1043\t    /// # Test Cases\n1044\t    ///\n1045\t    /// | Field | Input | Description |\n1046\t    /// |-------|-------|-------------|\n1047\t    /// | name | 123 | Integer where string needed |\n1048\t    /// | hostname | 8080 | Integer port as hostname |\n1049\t    /// | label | 3.14 | Float where string needed |\n1050\t    /// | description | 42 | Integer as description |\n1051\t    /// | path | 999 | Integer where path string needed |\n1052\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1053\t        (r#\"name: 123\"#, \"name\", \"123\", \"integer\"),\n1054\t        (r#\"hostname: 8080\"#, \"hostname\", \"8080\", \"integer\"),\n1055\t        (r#\"label: 3.14\"#, \"label\", \"3.14\", \"float\"),\n1056\t        (r#\"description: 42\"#, \"description\", \"42\", \"integer\"),\n1057\t        (r#\"path: 999\"#, \"path\", \"999\", \"integer\"),\n1058\t        (r#\"version: 1.0\"#, \"version\", \"1.0\", \"float\"),\n1059\t        (r#\"id: 0\"#, \"id\", \"0\", \"integer\"),\n1060\t        (r#\"title: 100\"#, \"title\", \"100\", \"integer\"),\n1061\t    ];\n1062\t\n1063\t    for (yaml, field_name, numeric_value, value_type) in test_cases {\n1064\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n1065\t        assert!(\n1066\t            value.is_ok(),\n1067\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for numeric {}\",\n1068\t            numeric_value\n1069\t        );\n1070\t\n1071\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n1072\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n1073\t\n1074\t        // Verify it's actually a number (not a string)\n1075\t        assert!(\n1076\t            !field_value.is_string(),\n1077\t            \"Field {} should not be string (got numeric {})\",\n1078\t            field_name,\n1079\t            numeric_value\n1080\t        );\n1081\t\n1082\t        // Verify it's a number\n1083\t        let is_number = if value_type == \"integer\" {\n1084\t            field_value.is_i64()\n1085\t        } else {\n1086\t            field_value.as_f64().is_some()\n1087\t        };\n1088\t        assert!(\n1089\t            is_number,\n1090\t            \"Field {} should be {} type (value: {})\",\n1091\t            field_name, value_type, numeric_value\n1092\t        );\n1093\t\n1094\t        // Verify conversion to string fails\n1095\t        let result = field_value.as_str();\n1096\t        assert!(\n1097\t            result.is_none(),\n1098\t            \"Numeric {} should not convert to string for field {}\",\n1099\t            numeric_value,\n1100\t            field_name\n1101\t        );\n1102\t\n1103\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n1104\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, \"string\", value_type);\n1105\t        assert!(\n1106\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1107\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} {} in string field {}\",\n1108\t            value_type,\n1109\t            numeric_value,\n1110\t            field_name\n1111\t        );\n1112\t\n1113\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n1114\t        assert!(\n1115\t            error_msg.contains(\"string\"),\n1116\t            \"Error should mention expected string type for {}\",\n1117\t            field_name\n1118\t        );\n1119\t    }\n1120\t}\n1121\t\n1122\t// ============================================================================\n1123\t// Type Mismatch: Expected Array/Slice But Got Scalar\n1124\t// ============================================================================\n1125\t\n1126\t#[test]\n1127\tfn test_expected_array_got_scalar() {\n1128\t    /// Test: Array fields receiving scalar values\n1129\t    ///\n1130\t    /// These test cases verify that scalar values (integers, strings, booleans)\n1131\t    /// are properly rejected when arrays/slices are expected.\n1132\t    ///\n1133\t    /// # Test Cases\n1134\t    ///\n1135\t    /// | Expected Type | Actual Input | Description |\n1136\t    /// |---------------|--------------|-------------|\n1137\t    /// | array | 42 | actual_type,\n1159\t            actual_value\n1160\t        );\n1161\t\n1162\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n1163\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n1164\t\n1165\t        // Verify field_name,\n1170\t            actual_type,\n1171\t            actual_value\n1172\t        );\n1173\t\n1174\t        // Verify it matches the expected actual type\n1175\t        let field_value.is_bool(),\n1179\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n1180\t            \"null\" => field_value.is_null(),\n1181\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected actual type: {}\", actual_type),\n1182\t        };\n1183\t        assert!(\n1184\t            matches_actual_type,\n1185\t            \"Field {} should be {} type (value: {})\",\n1186\t            field_name, actual_type, actual_value\n1187\t        );\n1188\t\n1189\t        // Verify conversion to sequence fails\n1190\t        let result = field_value.as_sequence();\n1191\t        assert!(\n1192\t            result.is_none(),\n1193\t            \"{} {} should not convert to sequence for field {}\",\n1194\t            actual_type,\n1195\t            actual_value,\n1196\t            field_name\n1197\t        );\n1198\t\n1199\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n1200\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, \"array\", actual_type);\n1201\t        assert!(\n1202\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1203\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} {} in array field {}\",\n1204\t            actual_type,\n1205\t            actual_value,\n1206\t            field_name\n1207\t        );\n1208\t\n1209\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n1210\t        assert!(\n1211\t ============================================================================\n1292\t// Edge Cases: Compatible But Wrong Types\n1293\t// ============================================================================\n1294\t\n1295\t#[test]\n1296\tfn test_integer_float_cross_conversion() {\n1297\t    /// Test: Integer vs Float cross-conversion issues\n1298\t    ///\n1299\t    /// These test cases verify that integers and floats are distinguished\n1300\t    /// even when they may appear compatible.\n1301\t    ///\n1302\t    /// # Test Cases\n1303\t    ///\n1304\t    /// | Expected | Actual | Input | Description |\n1305\t    /// |----------|--------|-------|-------------|\n1306\t    /// | integer | float | 3.14 | Float where integer needed |\n1307\t    /// | float | integer | 42 | Integer where float needed |\n1308\t    /// | integer | float | 1.0 | Whole number as float |\n1309\t    /// | float | integer | 0 | Zero as integer |\n1310\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1311\t        (r#\"port: 3.14\"#, \"port\", \"integer\", \"float\"),\n1312\t        (r#\"rate: 42\"#, \"rate\", \"float\", \"integer\"),\n1313\t        (r#\"count: 1.0\"#, \"count\", \"integer\", \"float\"),\n1314\t        (r#\"timeout: 0\"#, \"timeout\", \"float\", \"integer\"),\n1315\t        (r#\"size: 2.5\"#, \"size\", \"integer\", \"float\"),\n1316\t        (r#\"ratio: 100\"#, \"ratio\", \"float\", \"integer\"),\n1317\t    ];\n1318\t\n1319\t    for (yaml, field_name, expected_type, actual_type) in test_cases {\n1320\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n1321\t        assert!(\n1322\t            value.is_ok(),\n1323\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {} to {}\",\n1324\t            actual_type,\n1325\t            expected_type\n1326\t        );\n1327\t\n1328\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n1329\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n1330\t\n1331\t        // Verify actual type\n1332\t        let is_actual_type = if actual_type == \"integer\" {\n1333\t            field_value.is_i64()\n1334\t        } else {\n1335\t            field_value.as_f64().is_some()\n1336\t        };\n1337\t        assert!(\n1338\t            is_actual_type,\n1339\t            \"Field {} should be {} type\",\n1340\t            field_name, actual_type\n1341\t        );\n1342\t\n1343\t        // Verify conversion to expected type fails\n1344\t        let conversion_succeeds = if expected_type == \"integer\" {\n1345\t            field_value.as_i64().is_some()\n1346\t        } else {\n1347\t            field_value.as_f64().is_some()\n1348\t        };\n1349\t\n1350\t        // For float->integer conversion, we expect failure\n1351\t        // For integer->float conversion, we expect success in serde_yaml\n1352\t        // but should fail if strict type checking is required\n1353\t        if actual_type == \"float\" && expected_type == \"integer\" {\n1354\t            assert!(\n1355\t                !conversion_succeeds || field_value.as_f64().unwrap().fract() != 0.0,\n1356\t                \"Float with fractional part should not convert to integer for {}\",\n1357\t                field_name\n1358\t            );\n1359\t        }\n1360\t\n1361\t        // Verify type mismatch error can be created\n1362\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, expected_type, actual_type);\n1363\t        assert!(\n1364\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1365\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} to {} conversion\",\n1366\t            actual_type,\n1367\t            expected_type\n1368\t        );\n1369\t    }\n1370\t}\n1371\t\n1372\t#[test]\n1373\tfn }\n1584\t}\n1585\t\n1586\t#[test]\n1587\tfn test_incompatible_scalar_to_scalar_conversions() {\n1588\t    /// Test: Incompatible scalar-to-scalar conversions\n1589\t    ///\n1590\t    /// These test cases verify various scalar type mismatches.\n1591\t    ///\n1592\t    /// # Test Cases\n1593\t    ///\n1594\t    /// | Expected | Actual | Description |\n1595\t    /// |----------|--------|-------------|\n1596\t    /// | integer | boolean | Boolean to integer |\n1597\t    /// | boolean | string | String to boolean |\n1598\t    /// | float | boolean | Boolean to float |\n1599\t    /// | string | boolean | Boolean to string |\n1600\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1601\t        (r#\"port: true\"#, \"port\", \"integer\", \"boolean\"),\n1602\t        (r#\"enabled: \"yes\"\"#, \"enabled\", \"boolean\", \"string\"),\n1603\t        (r#\"rate: false\"#, \"rate\", \"float\", \"boolean\"),\n1604\t        (r#\"name: true\"#, \"name\", \"string\", \"boolean\"),\n1605\t        (r#\"count: \"42\"\"#, \"count\", \"integer\", \"string\"),\n1606\t        (r#\"flag: 1\"#, \"flag\", \"boolean\", \"integer\"),\n1607\t        (r#\"timeout: \"fast\"\"#, \"timeout\", \"integer\", \"string\"),\n1608\t        (r#\"ratio: true\"#, \"ratio\", \"float\", \"boolean\"),\n1609\t    ];\n1610\t\n1611\t    for (yaml, field_name, expected_type, actual_type) in test_cases {\n1612\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n1613\t        assert!(\n1614\t            value.is_ok(),\n1615\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {} -> {}\",\n1616\t            actual_type,\n1617\t            expected_type\n1618\t        );\n1619\t\n1620\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n1621\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n1622\t\n1623\t        // Verify actual type matches\n1624\t        let is_actual_type = match actual_type {\n1625\t            \"boolean\" => field_value.is_bool(),\n1626\t            \"string\" => field_value.is_string(),\n1627\t            \"integer\" => field_value.is_i64(),\n1628\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n1629\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected actual type: {}\", actual_type),\n1630\t        };\n1631\t        assert!(\n1632\t            is_actual_type,\n1633\t            \"Field {} should be {} type\",\n1634\t            field_name, actual_type\n1635\t        );\n1636\t\n1637\t        // Verify conversion to expected type fails\n1638\t        let conversion_succeeds = match expected_type {\n1639\t            \"boolean\" => field_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n1640\t            \"string\" => field_value.as_str().is_some(),\n1641\t            \"integer\" => field_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n1642\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n1643\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected_type),\n1644\t        };\n1645\t\n1646\t        assert!(\n1647\t            !conversion_succeeds,\n1648\t            \"{} should not convert to {} for field {}\",\n1649\t            actual_type, expected_type, field_name\n1650\t        );\n1651\t\n1652\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n1653\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, expected_type, actual_type);\n1654\t        assert!(\n1655\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1656\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} -> {} in field {}\",\n1657\t            actual_type,\n1658\t            expected_type,\n1659\t            field_name\n1660\t        );\n1661\t    }\n1662\t}\n1663\t\n1664\t// ============================================================================\n1665\t// Integer Overflow/Underflow Tests\n1666\t// ============================================================================\n1667\t\n1668\t#[test]\n1669\tfn test_integer_overflow_values() {\n1670\t    /// Test: Integer overflow scenarios\n1671\t    ///\n1672\t    /// These test cases verify that values exceeding the maximum representable\n1673\t    /// integer values are properly handled and produce appropriate errors.\n1674\t    ///\n1675\t    /// # Test Cases\n1676\t    ///\n1677\t    /// | Input | Description |\n1678\t    /// |-------|-------------|\n1679\t    /// | 9223372036854775808 | i64::MAX + 1 (overflow) |\n1680\t    /// | 9999999999999999999 | Arbitrary large number |\n1681\t    /// | 18446744073709551615 | u64::MAX (overflow for i64) |\n1682\t    /// | 100000000000000000000 | Extremely large value |\n1683\t    /// | 92233720368547758070 | 10x i64::MAX |\n1684\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1685\t        (\"9223372036854775808\", \"i64_MAX + 1\"),\n1686\t        (\"9999999999999999999\", \"arbitrary large number\"),\n1687\t        (\"18446744073709551615\", \"u64_MAX\"),\n1688\t        (\"100000000000000000000\", \"extremely large value\"),\n1689\t        (\"92233720368547758070\", \"10x i64_MAX\"),\n1690\t    ];\n1691\t\n1692\t    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n1693\t        let yaml = format!(\"port: {}\", input);\n1694\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n1695\t\n1696\t        // YAML may parse these as strings or integers depending on implementation\n1697\t        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n1698\t            let port_value = &parsed_value[\"port\"];\n1699\t\n1700\t            // If parsed as integer, verify it overflows or is clamped\n1701\t            if port_value.is_i64() {\n1702\t                let int_value = port_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n1703\t                // If YAML clamped to max, that's acceptable behavior\n1704\t                assert!(\n1705\t                    int_value <= i64::MAX,\n1706\t                    \"Integer should be at most i64::MAX for {}\",\n1707\t                    description\n1708\t                );\n1709\t            } else if port_value.is_string() {\n1710\t                // String representation - cannot convert to integer\n1711\t                let result = port_value.as_i64();\n1712\t                assert!(\n1713\t                    result.is_none(),\n1714\t                    \"Overflow string '{}' should not convert to integer ({})\",\n1715\t                    input,\n1716\t                    description\n1717\t                );\n1718\t\n1719\t                // Verify type mismatch error handling\n1720\t                let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"overflow\");\n1721\t                assert!(\n1722\t                    error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1723\t                    \"Type mismatch error should be created for overflow case {}\",\n1724\t                    description\n1725\t                );\n1726\t            } else if port_value.is_u64() {\n1727\t                // Some YAML parsers use u64 for large values\n1728\t                let u64_value = port_value.as_u64().unwrap();\n1729\t                assert!(\n1730\t                    u64_value > i64::MAX as u64,\n1731\t                    \"u64 value should exceed i64::MAX for {}\",\n1732\t                    description\n1733\t                );\n1734\t            }\n1735\t        }\n1736\t\n1737\t        // Verify error handling for overflow scenario\n1738\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"overflow\");\n1739\t        assert!(\n1740\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1741\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for overflow {}\",\n1742\t            description\n1743\t        );\n1744\t    }\n1745\t}\n1746\t\n1747\t#[test]\n1748\tfn test_integer_underflow_values() {\n1749\t    /// Test: Integer underflow test_negative_int16_to_uint16_conversions() {\n1894\t    /// Test: Negative int16 values cannot convert to uint16\n1895\t    ///\n1896\t    /// These test cases verify that negative int16 values are properly rejected\n1897\t    /// when attempting to convert them to uint16.\n1898\t    ///\n1899\t    /// # Test Cases\n1900\t    ///\n1901\t    /// | Input | Description |\n1902\t    /// |-------|-------------|\n1903\t    /// | -1 | Basic negative value |\n1904\t    /// | -128 | int8::MIN (common boundary) |\n1905\t    /// | -32768 | int16::MIN (minimum int16) |\n1906\t    /// | -32769 | int16::MIN - 1 (beyond int16 range) |\n1907\t    /// | -65535 | Large negative value |\n1908\t    /// | -65536 | Large negative value -1 |\n1909\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1910\t        (r#\"value: -1\"#, \"-1\", \"basic negative\"),\n1911\t        (r#\"value: -128\"#, \"-128\", \"int8 min\"),\n1912\t        (r#\"value: -32768\"#, \"-32768\", \"int16 min\"),\n1913\t        (r#\"value: -32769\"#, \"-32769\", \"int16 min - 1\"),\n1914\t        (r#\"value: -65535\"#, \"-65535\", \"large negative -65535\"),\n1915\t        (r#\"value: -65536\"#, \"-65536\", \"large negative -65536\"),\n1916\t    ];\n1917\t\n1918\t    for (yaml, value_str, description) in test_cases {\n1919\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n1920\t        assert!(\n1921\t            value.is_ok(),\n1922\t            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n1923\t            description\n1924\t        );\n1925\t\n1926\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n1927\t        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n1928\t\n1929\t        // Verify it's a negative integer\n1930\t        assert!(\n1931\t            field_value.is_i64(),\n1932\t            \"Field should be i64 ({})\",\n1933\t            description\n1934\t        );\n1935\t        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n1936\t        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Field should be negative ({})\", description);\n1937\t\n1938\t        // Verify the actual value matches expected\n1939\t        assert_eq!(\n1940\t            int_value,\n1941\t            value_str.parse::<i64>().unwrap(),\n1942\t            \"Field value should match expected {} ({})\",\n1943\t            value_str,\n1944\t            description\n1945\t        );\n1946\t\n1947\t        // For uint16 conversion simulation - !fits_in_uint16,\n1952\t            \"Negative value {} should not fit in uint16 ({})\",\n1953\t            value_str, description\n1954\t        );\n1955\t\n1956\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created for uint16 context\n1957\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint16\", \"int16_negative\");\n1958\t        assert!(\n1959\t            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1960\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for negative {} in uint16 context ({})\",\n1961\t            value_str,\n1962\t            description\n1963\t        );\n1964\t\n1965\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n1966\t        assert!(\n1967\t            error_msg.contains(\"uint16\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n1968\t            \"Error should mention uint16/unsigned type for {}\",\n1969\t            description\n1970\t        );\n1971\t        assert!(\n1972\t            error_msg.contains(\"negative\") || error_msg.contains(\"int16\"),\n1973\t            \"Error should mention negative/int16 for {}\",\n1974\t            description\n1975\t        );\n1976\t    }\n1977\t}\n1978\t\n1979\t#[test]\n1980\tfn test_negative_int8_to_uint8_conversions() {\n1981\t    /// Test: Negative int8 values cannot convert to uint8\n1982\t    ///\n1983\t    /// These test cases
